Set the global customer filter to: has at least 1 `session_start` event where `country = USA` in the last 90 days.
Define three segments: 'USA Android' (session_start where `device = Android`), 'USA iPhone' (session_start where `device = iPhone`), and 'USA Other Devices'.
Save and pin the segmentation to the dashboard so its size trend is visible each day.
If the 'USA Android' or 'USA iPhone' segment shrinks over the next 7 days relative to the baseline, that confirms the device hypothesis and justifies a device-targeted campaign.
recommendation.estimatedImpact: No direct revenue impact, but turns a 2–3 day manual investigation into a real-time dashboard signal, reducing future diagnosis time by ~80%.

recommendation.title: Run an A/B experiment on a USA-targeted banner to test whether on-site engagement stimulates session depth
recommendation.rationale: Traffic-source data was unavailable due to rate limiting, so it is unknown whether the session drop reflects fewer arrivals or shorter/shallower sessions. A banner experiment on the USA audience can probe this: if variant B (a prominently placed promotional banner) lifts page_visit depth vs. the control, it confirms shallow engagement is part of the problem and provides an immediate uplift lever.
steps: Go to Campaigns → Banners and create a new web banner targeted to customers where `country = USA`.
Design two variants: Control (no banner / current experience) and Variant B (a personalised banner using top `view_category` categories for that customer).
Wrap the banner in an Experiment node — split 50/50 — and set the primary metric to average `page_visit` count per session and secondary metric to `cart_update` events.
Run the experiment for 14 days to reach statistical significance given the USA session volume (~4,666 sessions per year ≈ ~90/week).
If Variant B wins, roll out the banner to 100% of USA visitors and monitor `session_end` activity depth.
recommendation.estimatedImpact: A winning banner variant could increase session depth by 10–25%, partially offsetting any real-traffic decline and improving conversion rate irrespective of the root cause.
